back_translation: Therefore, now there is no verdict of condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus
For the principle of the (royal) Spirit of life in Christ Jesus has set you free from the principle of sin and death

For what the Law was unable to do — because it was weak through the flesh — God did, by sending His own Son in the form of flesh that has sin, and as a sin-offering, He (royally) condemned sin in the flesh
So that the righteous regulation of the Law might be fulfilled in us — those who do not walk according to the flesh, but according to the (royal) Spirit
For those who exist according to the flesh aim their thoughts at the things of the flesh — but those who exist according to the (royal) Spirit aim at the things of the (royal) Spirit
For the mind of the flesh is death — but the mind of the (royal) Spirit is life and peace
Because the mind of the flesh is hostile toward God — for it does not submit to the Law of God — and indeed cannot submit
And those who exist in the flesh cannot make God (royally) pleased
But you are not in the flesh, but in the (royal) Spirit — if indeed the (royal) Spirit of God dwells in you. And if anyone does not have the (royal) Spirit of Christ, that one is not of Him
But if Christ (royally) dwells in you, the body is dead because of sin — but the spirit is alive because of righteousness

And if the (royal) Spirit of the One who (royally) raised Jesus from the dead dwells in you, the One who (royally) raised Jesus Christ from the dead will also (royally) give life to your mortal bodies, through His (royal) Spirit who (royally) dwells within you
Therefore, brothers, we are debtors — not debtors to the flesh, to live according to the flesh
For if you live according to the flesh, you will die — but if by the (royal) Spirit you put-to-death the deeds of the body, you will live
For all whom the (royal) Spirit of God (royally) leads, those people are sons of God
For you did not receive a spirit of slavery, returning to fear — but you received the (royal) Spirit of the right-of-being-a-son, by whom we cry out: 'Abba — Father'
The (royal) Spirit Himself bears witness together with our spirit that we are children of God
And if children, then heirs as well — heirs of God, and co-heirs with Christ — if indeed we suffer together with Him truly, in order that we may also receive glory together with Him
For I count that the sufferings of this present time are not comparable to the (royal) glory that will be (royally) revealed in us
For the eager-craning-watching of the creation is awaiting the revelation of the sons of God
For the creation was made-subject to emptiness, not by its own willing, but through the One who (royally) made-subject — in hope
That the creation itself will be set free from the slavery of decay — into the freedom of the (royal) glory of the children of God
For we know that all creation groans together, and travails together as in childbirth, until now
Not only that — we ourselves, who have the firstfruits of the (royal) Spirit, also groan within ourselves, awaiting the right-of-being-a-son — namely, the redemption of our body
For we received salvation in this hope — but hope that is already seen is not hope — for who hopes for what he already sees?
But if we hope for what we do not yet see, we await it patiently with perseverance

In the same way, the (royal) Spirit also (royally) helps our weakness — for we do not know what we ought to pray, but the (royal) Spirit Himself (royally) intercedes for us with groans that cannot be expressed in words
And the One who (royally) searches the hearts (royally) knows the mind of the (royal) Spirit — because the (royal) Spirit (royally) intercedes for the saints according to the (royal) will of God
And we know that, for those who love God, all things (royally) work together for good — for those who are (royally) called according to His (royal) purpose
For those whom He (royally) foreknew, He also (royally) predestined to be conformed to the (royal) image of His Son — so that the Son might (royally) be the firstborn among many brothers

And those whom He (royally) predestined, He also (royally) called — and those whom He (royally) called, He also (royally) declared righteous — and those whom He (royally) declared righteous, He also (royally) gave glory
Therefore, what shall we say to these things? If God (royally) is on our side, who can be against us?
He who did not (royally) hold-back-and-spare His own Son, but (royally) handed Him over for us all — how will He not also (royally) give us all things together with Him?
Who will bring a charge against those whom God (royally) chose? God (royally) is the One who declares righteous

Who then will judge to condemn? Christ is the One who (royally) died — moreover, who (royally) was raised — who (royally) is seated at the right (royal) hand of God — and who (royally) intercedes for us
Who will separate us from the love of Christ? Affliction, distress, persecution, famine, nakedness, danger, or sword?
Just as it is written: 'For Your sake, we are killed all day long — we are reckoned as sheep that will be slaughtered'
But in all these things, we are conquerors-beyond-victors, through the One who (royally) loved us
For I am convinced that — neither death nor life, neither angels nor authorities, neither things in the present nor things to come, nor any powers
Neither height nor depth, nor any other created thing, will be able to separate us from the love of God which is in Christ Jesus our Lord
